title: viola × vermontana
intro: Viola × vermontana, also known as the hybrid blue violet, is a plant species that is a cross between V. sororia and V. pubescens. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Viola × vermontana include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of colds and fever. Herbal preparations of Viola × vermontana can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
